name: Floor and Ceil in Sorted Array
statement: Given a sorted array nums and an integer x. Find the floor and ceil of x in nums. The floor of x is the largest element in the array which is smaller than or equal to x. The ceiling of x is the smallest element in the array greater than or equal to x. If no floor or ceil exists, output -1.
constraints:
•   1 <= nums.length <= 105 
  •   0 < nums[i], x < 105 
  •   nums is sorted in ascending order.

examples.explanation: The floor of 5 in the array is 4, and the ceiling of 5 in the array is 7.
The floor of 8 in the array is 8, and the ceiling of 8 in the array is also 8.
